subject: [PATCH] btrfs: don't force DIO writes to be serialized

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Reviewer David Sterba requested more information about the fio script and the block group profile used for DIO writes, indicating a need for additional context before fully evaluating the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
That's quite an improvement. What's the actual fio script you've used?
Also the DIO depends on the block group profile wrt the buffered
fallback so that would be good to know too.

2026-04-22.patch_summary: This patch addresses an issue in the Btrfs file system where Direct I/O (DIO) writes are being serialized due to a missing flag that was set before the transition to the new mount API in 2023. The flag, SB_NOSEC, tells the VFS that the filesystem may have files without security xattrs, allowing optimizations. Without this flag, DIO writes to the same file will be locked exclusively, leading to reduced performance. This patch removes the serialization by not forcing DIO writes to be exclusive locks, resulting in a significant improvement in DIO throughput, as demonstrated by fio benchmark results showing a 59% increase.

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary: Reviewer Qu Wenruo suggested using the iomap bounded page solution to avoid falling back to buffered IO and instead use an extra page copy, which would eliminate the need for btrfs' specific fallback checks.
reviews.raw_body:
There is the bounded page solution from iomap already, which will no 
longer fallback to buffered IO but to use extra page copy to make sure 
the final bio won't change its content halfway.

IIRC it's one extra flag and remove the btrfs' specific fallback checks, 
but I haven't yet verified the behavior/code.

Thanks,
Qu

reviews.author: Mark Harmstone (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to Qu Wenruo's feedback, stating that the patch addresses a different issue and is focused on releasing the inode rwsem exclusively for each write.
reviews.raw_body:
That sounds like a different thing - this is just making it so that
we're not forced to take the inode rwsem exclusively for each write.

2026-04-23.patch_summary: This patch addresses a performance issue in the Btrfs file system where DIO (Direct I/O) writes are being serialized, leading to a significant drop-off in write performance. The problem was observed on a VM with 8 cores and 8GB of RAM, where XFS and ext4 showed much better performance (~3GB/s). To fix this issue, the patch aims to prevent DIO writes from being unnecessarily serialized, allowing for improved write throughput.

reviews.author: David Sterba
reviews.summary: Reviewer David Sterba requested that a test case be added to fstests/tests/perf, specifically the fio test used to demonstrate DIO write performance issues in BTRFS.
reviews.raw_body:
As you've used fio for that, please add the test case to the
fstests/tests/perf. Though the 'pref' tests haven't caught up I'd like
to at least collect them in our btrfs/fstests repository.

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch addresses a performance issue in the Btrfs file system where DIO (Direct I/O) writes are being serialized, leading to a significant drop-off in write performance. The problem was observed on a VM with 8 cores and 8GB of RAM, where XFS and ext4 showed much better performance (~3GB/s). To fix this issue, the patch aims to prevent DIO writes from being unnecessarily serialized, allowing for improved write throughput.
